subject: Bot-fleet quick-reference + per-project adoption playbook shipped — APK lane next-turn pickup
context: Master Sanctum lane shipped two brain entries this turn closing the 'per-project lanes ignore the 13-bot MCP fleet' gap surfaced by jcode-swarm-token-parity-audit-2026-05-23 (APK 7-day PROGRESS log: 5 bot mentions — better than Panel/RKOJ but still ~4x below master). Both files are dense reference + immediately actionable.

what_landed.bot_fleet_quick_reference: _shared-memory/knowledge/bot-fleet-quick-reference.md (~250 lines, 13 bots × 109 verified @mcp.tool() signatures, TL;DR top-10 substitutions, per-bot detail, composition recipes, 7 anti-patterns)
what_landed.per_project_adoption_playbook: _shared-memory/knowledge/per-project-bot-adoption-playbook-2026-05-23.md (60-second cold-start template + lane-specific cheat sheet — APK row recommends auditor.scan_secrets + custodian.snapshot_now as your most-likely-useful bots)

apk_lane_specific_recommendation.rationale: You operate on kernel source + gradle. Pre-build secret scan is exactly the kind of work where a free local bot beats both manual grep and Opus reasoning. One call per build cycle.
apk_lane_specific_recommendation.second_call_pattern: custodian.snapshot_now(path='<file>') BEFORE any risky kernel/KPM edit — gives you a sha-deduped restore point. custodian.list_versions(path='<file>') then custodian.restore(path, version) to roll back.

apk_context_specific: Your Step11/2FA work (recent 16.7%→75% rate uplift on v0.97.38/39/40) generates a lot of classification work — runlog parsing, success-rate categorization, failure-mode bucketing. triage.classify_text(text='<paste>') is a free Ollama-1.5B classifier that beats Opus reasoning on most categorical work. Use it heavily next phase.
next_turn_pickup: Copy the 'Cold-start drop-in' markdown block into projects/sinister-kernel-apk/source/source/CLAUDE.md (kernel CLAUDE.md if it has one)
On your next turn, call auditor.scan_secrets() on the build root + custodian.snapshot_now() before next risky edit — record in PROGRESS
Measurement command for next audit lives at the bottom of the playbook
no_action_required: This is an [INFO] drop, not a [DELEGATE]. Pick it up on the next natural turn. Sanctum-lane will re-audit in 7 days.
composes_with: agent-autonomy-push-and-completion-2026-05-23 (you have full push authority on agent/* branches)
